reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output document is missing a significant portion of the source content. Specifically, pages 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, and 23 are entirely absent from the 'sections' array, replaced by an omission placeholder. This results in the loss of approximately 72% of the document's pages and core information, including all detailed use cases and integration scenarios.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Pages 4 through 6, 8 through 13, and 15 through 23 of the source PDF, containing detailed information on OneDrive for Business, SharePoint Online, collaboration features, use cases, best practices, and integration scenarios.
concerns.location: Sections array, element index 4 (between reading_order 3 and 7)

concerns.analysis: The output fails to preserve the heading hierarchy of the source. Sub-headings present on multiple slides (e.g., 'OneDrive Storage' on page 7, 'Folder Structure & Naming' on page 14) are not captured as semantic heading levels (such as H3 or H4). Instead, they are either placed into table header cells or merged as plain text within data cells. This prevents screen reader users from navigating the document's internal section structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 7 contains sub-headings 'OneDrive Storage' and 'SharePoint Storage'. Page 14 contains sub-headings 'Folder Structure & Naming', 'Sharing Governance', etc.
concerns.output_quote: In the 'Storage Scope' section, sub-headings are mapped to table headers (cell_type: header). In the 'Best Practices' section (page 14), sub-headings are merged into plain text blocks within data cells (e.g., "text": "Folder Structure & Naming\nMaintain an organized...").

concerns.analysis: The output introduces tables where none exist in the source document. Pages 7 and 14 utilize a multi-column or grid-like layout for comparisons and lists, but these are not formatted as tables in the PDF. The pipeline has forced this content into a table structure, which changes the semantic organization and reading experience compared to the original document structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 7 ('Storage Scope') and Page 14 ('Best Practices') contain formatted blocks of text and headings, not tables.
concerns.output_quote: The output generates a table element for both the 'Storage Scope' section and the 'Best Practices' section.
concerns.location: Sections for 'Storage Scope' (page 7, index 7) and 'Best Practices' (page 14, index 14)

concerns.analysis: Several meaningful images, diagrams, and icons present in the source PDF are either missing entirely or replaced by large green placeholder boxes in the HTML output. For instance, the collaboration icons on page 8 and the workflow images on page 21 are replaced by green blocks. Additionally, complex diagrams like the network visualization on pages 11 and 20, the folder structure diagram on page 9, and the QR code on page 24 are missing. concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 8: three illustrative icons for collaboration; Page 11/20: network diagram; Page 21: three workflow-related images; Page 24: QR code for follow-up.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML shows three large green rectangles under 'Collaboration' and 'Power Automate Flows'. The network diagram and QR code are entirely absent from their respective sections.
concerns.location: Collaboration section, Integration & Extensibility section, Power Automate Flows section, and NC DIT Innovation Summit Follow-Up section.

action_items: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.
